steps.explain: The triangle inequality theorem requires the sum of any two sides to be greater than the third. With sides 7 and 10 and unknown side \(x\), set up: \(7+10>x\), \(7+x>10\), and \(10+x>7\).
From \(7+10>x\): \(x<17\). From \(7+x>10\): \(x>3\). The third inequality is always satisfied for positive \(x\).

Evaluate choices:
  • A (\(x<3\)): gives only values too small — eliminated.
  • B (\(x>17\)): gives only values too large — eliminated.
  • C (\(3C is correct.
  • D (\(x<3\) or \(x>17\)): these are the excluded regions, not the valid range — eliminated.
